hypothesis: Prices that deviate significantly from their recent mean (beyond 2 standard deviations) tend to revert. This is strongest in liquid, range-bound equities and ETFs where fundamentals haven't changed.
universe: Liquid large-cap equities or sector ETFs
entry_logic: Buy when price touches or crosses below the lower Bollinger Band (mean - 2*std). Short when price touches upper band.
exit_logic: Exit when price returns to the middle band (SMA). Or: time-based exit after N days if mean reversion hasn't occurred.
position_sizing: Size inversely proportional to distance from the band (larger position = closer to extreme).
risk_management: Hard stop at 3x standard deviation. Max holding period of 10 days. No adding to losers.
known_failure_modes: Trending markets: price can stay beyond bands for extended periods, causing large losses.
Earnings/news events: fundamental shifts make mean reversion invalid.
Works better on portfolios than individual names (diversification of mean reversion bets).

historical_performance.notes: Significantly better on a basket of 20+ liquid equities than on single names.
